title: Multi-Lens Evaluator Panel
description: Lets the qualitative evaluator step run a panel of narrow-lens LLM judges (correctness, security, regression, quality) instead of one general judge, reconciling them by majority vote (fail-closed on ties) via the already-built reconcile() reducer. Opt-in per config; off by default it is byte-identical to today's single-judge behavior. This is the near-term, workflow-independent payoff extracted from the dormant workflow engine: it works in CI, headless, and /bober-run today.

resolvedClarifications.answer: Core + polish, 2 sprints: Sprint 1 ships the working capability (config + lens-aware eval + reconcile wiring); Sprint 2 adds the built-in lens prompt catalog, per-lens telemetry, and tests.

Byte-identical to today when the panel is OFF: panel.enabled=false OR lenses.length<2 runs the single existing agent eval call unchanged (same prompt, same one EvalResult, same combine logic). Zero behavior/cost change unless opted in.

assumptions: The single qualitative judge lives in runAgentEvaluation() (src/orchestrator/evaluator-agent.ts:134); it is called once from runEvaluatorAgent (src/orchestrator/evaluator-agent.ts:86) and combined via passed = programmaticEval.passed && agentResult.passed (src/orchestrator/evaluator-agent.ts:112). The panel changes only how the one agentResult is produced — the combine, programmatic eval, and retry loop are untouched.

Config gets evaluator.panel: { enabled: boolean=false, lenses: string[]=[], maxConcurrent: number=4 } in EvaluatorSectionSchema (src/config/schema.ts:104), mirrored in createDefaultConfig (src/config/schema.ts:375).
The reducer is reused as-is from src/orchestrator/workflow/reconciler.ts. Its sprintId/round args do not affect output, so passing contract.contractId + an iteration/round number is safe.

Panel fans out with a bounded Promise.all (<= maxConcurrent); each lens is a focused variant of the existing evaluator prompt built in runAgentEvaluation. programmaticEval (typecheck/lint/build/test via src/evaluators/registry.ts:171 runEvaluation) is unchanged and still runs once.

Off-path guarantee: panel.enabled=false OR lenses.length<2 runs the existing single agent call verbatim. There is no existing evaluator-agent unit test, so the guarantee is proven by a NEW test that mocks the LLM client and asserts exactly one judge call on the off path.
The evaluator LLM call uses createClient (src/orchestrator/evaluator-agent.ts ~153); tests mock createClient / the client so no real LLM is invoked.
Per-lens telemetry uses the existing emit() (src/telemetry/emit.ts:69) and appendHistory; the reducer already retains lensVerdicts to surface.
outOfScope: Anything touching the workflow engine, .claude/workflows, or the dormant invoke() — this is the TS evaluator path only.
Changing the programmatic evaluators, the combine logic (programmaticEval.passed && agentResult.passed), the retry loop, or the EvaluationRunResult shape.
Per-lens weighting or non-majority voting schemes (majority / fail-closed only, per the reducer).
Parallelizing across sprints or any cost-budgeting beyond the maxConcurrent cap.
Wiring lenses into the skill-driven (Claude-in-session) evaluator or the agents/*.md definitions.

features.title: Panel config and reconcile wiring
features.description: Add the opt-in evaluator.panel config and make the qualitative evaluator fan out into a lens panel reconciled by the existing reducer, with byte-identical behavior when off.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: When evaluator.panel is unset or enabled=false, runAgentEvaluation makes exactly one judge LLM call and returns the same EvalResult shape as today (verified by a client-mocked unit test).
AC2: When panel.enabled=true with >=2 lenses, runAgentEvaluation issues one judge call per lens (bounded by maxConcurrent) and returns reconcile()'s canonical evaluator='panel' EvalResult.
AC3: A 2-pass / 2-fail lens panel reconciles to passed=false (fail-closed), and the reconciled result flows unchanged into passed = programmaticEval.passed && agentResult.passed.

features.title: Lens catalog and per-lens telemetry
features.description: Provide a built-in lens prompt catalog and surface each lens's verdict via telemetry/history so failures are attributable to a specific lens.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: Built-in lenses correctness/security/regression/quality resolve to distinct focus prompt fragments; an unknown custom lens string falls back to a generic focus without error.
AC2: When the panel runs, each lens's pass/fail verdict is emitted to history/telemetry (attributable by lens name).
AC3: The off-by-default guarantee and fan-out bounding are covered by tests; the full suite stays green.

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: TypeScript strict and ESLint produce zero errors across changed files; ESM .js specifiers and consistent-type-imports respected.
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: Run npx tsc --noEmit and npx eslint src/; both exit 0.

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: Default (panel off) evaluator behavior is unchanged — exactly one judge call, same combine.
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: Client-mocked unit test asserts a single judge call on the off path; full vitest suite stays green with no new failures beyond the documented flaky tool-count baseline.

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: Panel fan-out never exceeds evaluator.panel.maxConcurrent concurrent judge calls.
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: Unit test with a counting/mocked client asserts peak concurrency <= maxConcurrent.

integrationPoints: src/orchestrator/evaluator-agent.ts (runAgentEvaluation — the single judge call to fan out)
techNotes.dataModel: No new persistent shapes. Reuses EvalResult / EvaluationRunResult (frozen). evaluator.panel is a new config sub-object only.
securityConsiderations: No SDK leakage outside providers/ adapters.
A 'security' lens is a prompt focus only — it does not change tool permissions.
techNotes.existingPatterns: Mirror the existing runAgentEvaluation prompt assembly and createClient usage. Reuse reconcile() verbatim. Add config via Zod with a safe default (off). Collocate *.test.ts with Vitest; mock createClient to avoid real LLM calls.

metadata.riskNotes: Additive, opt-in, off by default. Main risk is preserving byte-identical off-path behavior, gated by a client-mocked single-call test. Reuses an already-tested reducer.
